privacy_policy: All entries use public URLs only. No individual wallet balances, private task content, or de-anonymization data. Project-scoped artifacts use sanitized placeholders.

entries.reuse_instructions: Clone the gist. Run `pytest test_guardrails.py` to execute all 59 tests. Add new fixtures to cases.json following the existing schema. Each fixture needs: id, name, category, input, expected.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Run the 59 tests locally and verify all pass. Add 3 new fixtures covering a category not yet tested (e.g., rate limiting, auth boundary).
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT rebuild RAG reliability or prompt-injection fixtures from scratch. Extend this pack instead. Check for overlap with scope-regression-fixtures.

entries.reuse_instructions: Import fixtures.json into any classifier test harness. Use expected-results.json for quick pass/fail. Validate new fixtures against fixtures.schema.json. See README for decision tree and consumption patterns.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Build a classifier function that passes all 20 fixtures. Report accuracy on boundary pairs specifically.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Covers: deprecated, duplicate, stale, off-grid, adversarial, policy_violation, unverifiable. Do NOT create separate 'task quality' fixtures without checking overlap here first.

entries.reuse_instructions: Use the schema to structure new alpha briefs. Four brief types supported: event_trade, structural_thesis, strategy_observation, intelligence_signal. Include machine_checks and agent_actionability_path for each brief.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Write a new alpha brief using the schema for a current market event. Validate against the JSON schema and include all required fields.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT create a new 'alpha format' or 'trade thesis template' without referencing this schema. Extend the brief_type enum if needed.

entries.reuse_instructions: Dashboard consumes scan-results.json. To add tickers: edit the THEMES dict in run_live_scan.py. Scoring formula: convergence × (1/IV) × log(liquidity). Requires IBKR Gateway for live data or Yahoo Finance fallback.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Run the scanner against 5 new tickers not in the current theme list. Verify scoring output matches expected formula. Report any data gaps.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT build a separate 'options screener' or 'LLM stock picker'. Extend this scanner's theme list and scoring weights instead.

entries.reuse_instructions: Consumes graph.json, audit.json, health.json, subs.json from the pft-validator repo. Data exported via cron scripts in pf-scout-bot/deploy/. To add a new data panel: add export logic to the relevant export script, update the dashboard HTML.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Verify that the hustlers section shows correct first-transaction-based account ages. Cross-reference 3 addresses against public ledger data.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT build a separate 'network dashboard' or 'contributor tracker'. This is the canonical visualization layer. Extend it.

entries.reuse_instructions: Daily automated export via cron. Source data from chain-index.db (SQLite). Sections: Pulse, Flow, Airdrops, Watch, Movers, Deep Cut. JSON export at pft-validator/lens/herald.json. Herald v2 snapshot format in development.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Verify one day's Herald data against raw public ledger queries. Confirm memo counts, airdrop totals, and contributor activity match.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT build a separate 'daily network report' or 'chain newsletter'. This is the canonical daily intelligence product. Contribute sections or verify data instead.

entries.reuse_instructions: On-chain subscription detection via amount-matching (keystone-encrypted memos). 2.5% protocol fee. Bot monitors XRPL for matching payments. Registry at pft-validator/lens/subs.json. To add a new subscription tier: update the SUBS bot payment detection logic.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Verify that the SUBS page shows correct subscriber counts. Cross-reference 2 subscriptions against public ledger transaction data.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT build a separate 'payment subscription' system. This is the canonical on-chain subscription primitive. Extend tiers or access controls instead.

entries.reuse_instructions: Human-first explainer with three contribution tiers, mental model section, jargon translation table, 10 social posts, 20 reply-ready community lines, PFT vs XRP comparison. Reuse social posts directly. Adapt mental models for different audiences.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Take 3 social posts from the pack, post them in a crypto community, and report engagement/questions received. Document which framing resonated.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT write a new 'what is Post Fiat' explainer from scratch. Use this as the base and adapt for specific audiences (developers, traders, researchers).

entries.reuse_instructions: Weekly-updatable template with JSON sidecar. Sources: explorer.testnet.postfiat.org (validators), postfiatorg GitHub (repos), public ledger (contributors/transactions). Includes adoption funnel, contamination register, confidence tiers.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Update the snapshot with current week's data. Verify validator count against live explorer. Note any metrics that have changed significantly.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT create a new 'network health dashboard' or 'weekly report' without checking this exists. Update this snapshot rather than creating parallel metrics.

entries.reuse_instructions: Community-maintained reference repo. Add projects to community/projects.md. Update weekly metrics in metrics/. Contribute resources to resources/. All PRs welcome. See CONTRIBUTING.md for rules.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Verify all links in the README still resolve. Add one new community project or resource that's missing. Submit PR.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: This IS the community reference index. Do NOT create a separate 'Post Fiat resources' repo. Contribute here instead.

entries.reuse_instructions: Community-maintained onboarding guide for Task Node setup and usage. GitHub Pages deployment. To contribute: fork the repo, edit markdown, submit PR.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Follow the onboarding guide end-to-end on a fresh machine. Document any steps that are unclear, outdated, or missing.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT write a new 'how to use Task Node' guide. Fix or extend this one instead.

entries.reuse_instructions: X25519/XChaCha20 encryption, IPFS pinning, pf.ptr:v4 pointer memos, tiered access manifests. CLI tools: pft-index validate, pft-index digest, pft-index encrypt. Schema: pft.index.snapshot.v1. Use for any subscriber-gated content publishing.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Create a minimal snapshot artifact, validate it against the schema, compute its digest. Document the workflow and any issues encountered.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT build encrypted publishing, IPFS pinning, or subscriber gating from scratch. This exists. Extend with new schemas (e.g., pft.intelligence.v1) or new content types.

entries.reuse_instructions: Real LLM captures from GPT, Gemini, Grok across themes (quantum, GLP-1, nuclear, AI infra). Each capture includes: model, query, timestamp, ranked recommendations. Use as seed data for convergence scoring or as training examples for signal extraction.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Run the same queries against current LLM versions. Compare rankings to the corpus captures. Document any convergence shifts.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT create a new 'LLM stock picks dataset' without referencing this corpus. Add new captures here rather than starting fresh.

entries.reuse_instructions: Deterministic reducer for reviewer queue prioritization. 34 tests. Classifies tasks by urgency, age, and reviewer load. Import the reducer logic to prioritize any queue of pending reviews.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Run the 34 tests locally. Feed 5 synthetic review items through the reducer and verify priority ordering matches expected output.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT build separate 'review prioritization' logic. Use or extend this reducer.

entries.reuse_instructions: Caps rewards based on evidence quality tiers. 35 tests. Three proof layers: existence, relevance, completion. Import the scoring logic to evaluate any task submission's evidence strength.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Run the 35 tests. Create 3 synthetic submissions with varying evidence quality and verify the reward cap adjusts correctly.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT build separate 'reward scaling' or 'evidence scoring' without checking this reducer. It defines the canonical three-layer proof model.

entries.reuse_instructions: Identifies patterns in stalled/abandoned tasks. Categories: scope creep, missing prerequisites, external dependency, unclear acceptance criteria. Use the pattern taxonomy to pre-screen new tasks before issuance.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Apply the stalled-task taxonomy to 5 currently open tasks. Classify each by risk category and suggest mitigations.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Do NOT create a new 'why tasks fail' analysis without referencing this triage. The taxonomy is established.

entries.reuse_instructions: Maps friction points in the task lifecycle from issuance to reward. Includes peer feedback. Use to identify where new contributors are likely to get stuck and design smoother pathways.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Walk a new contributor through their first task. Document where they hit friction points and compare against this triage's predictions.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Overlaps with stalled-task-triage but focuses on UX friction rather than structural failure. Check both before creating 'onboarding improvement' tasks.

entries.reuse_instructions: Converts Herald daily JSON into pft.index.snapshot.v1 format. 6 intelligence sections mapped as positions. Pending: pft.intelligence.v1 schema proposal (GitHub issue drafted on agtico/pft_indexing). Use as reference for converting any daily intelligence product into the indexing schema.
entries.suggested_reproduction_task: Run the converter against a sample Herald JSON. Verify output validates against pft.index.snapshot.v1 schema. Document fields that need the intelligence schema extension.
entries.duplicate_risk_notes: Active development. Do NOT build a separate 'Herald to IPFS' pipeline. This will become the canonical path once the intelligence schema is accepted.
